title: Disaster Related Cash Grants - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Disaster Services > Disaster Related Cash Grants 3 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF BUILDING CODES DIVISION FIRE HARDENING GRANT PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a grant program for fire hardening (fire resistant) building improvements to eligible structures damaged or lost in a wildfire since 2020. The grant program has incentives up to $6,000 and will be open until Monday, December 30, 2025 8am-5pm. 1535 Edgewater Street NW Salem, OR 97304 Eligibility: -Individuals whose home or business was lost or damaged in a wildfire since 2020 -Eligibility in the program will be determined by the applicant's county's building department, not by the Building Codes Division. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 378-4113 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HOUSING AND COMMUNITY SERVICES HOMEOWNER ASSISTANCE AND RECONSTRUCTION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance to homeowners whose properties were damaged or destroyed by wildfires and/or straight-line winds in 2020. Assistance may include: -Repairing an originally damaged or replacement stick-built or manufactured home -Building a new stick-built home on owned or leased land -Replacing a damaged manufactured home on land a homeowner owns, leases, or will secure a lease for -Purchasing a stick-built or manufactured home that is already installed Cannot reimburse any money a homeowner has already spent to repair or restore their home. New owners of a home damaged in the disaster are not eligible for HARP assistance. Cannot assist with second homes, foreclosed homes, recreational vehicles, campers, detached structures, housing units that are not permitted, mixed-use buildings, and multiplexes with five or more units. Eligibility: -Homeowners whose home was damaged or destroyed due to wildfires and/or straight-line winds in 2020 -Have remaining construction repairs of at least $3,000 -Home must be primary residence -Must be in good standing or payment plan with mortgage and property taxes Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 250-0938 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURE FARM SERVICE AGENCY TREE ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance to replant or rehabilitate eligible trees, bushes, and vines lost by natural disasters. No disaster declaration is required. 1400 Independence Avenue SW Stop 0506 Washington, DC 205200506 Eligibility: Orchardists and nursery tree growers who have experienced a 15% unpreventable loss of a stand of trees, bushes, or vines due to a natural disaster. Hours: Not applicable Get Directions Visit Website More Details BUILDING CODES DIVISION FIRE HARDENING GRANT PROGRAM
